content:
The steel angle section shown in the figure has elastic section modulus of 150.92 cm3 about the horizontal X−X axis, which passes through the centroid of the section.

The shape factor of the section is ______ (rounded off to 2 decimal places).
The first moment of area about the axis of bending for a beam cross-section is
Polar moment of inertia $$\left( {{{\rm I}_p}} \right),$$  in $$c{m^4},$$ of a rectangular section having width, $$b=2$$ $$cm$$ and depth $$d=6$$ $$cm$$ is __________

A linearly elastic beam of length 2l with flexural rigidity EI has negligible mass. A massless spring with a spring constant k and a rigid block of mass m are attached to the beam as shown in the figure.

The natural frequency of this system is
Consider the horizontal axis passing through the centroid of the steel beam cross-section shown in the figure. What is the shape factor (rounded off to one decimal place) for the cross-section?
explanation:
The elastic section modulus, $ Z_e $, is calculated as follows :

$ Z_e = \frac{I}{Y_{\max}}, \quad Y_{\max} = \frac{3b}{2} $

Now, compute the second moment of area, $ I_{xx} $, as :

$ \begin{aligned} I_{xx} &= \frac{3b \times b^3}{12} + 2 \times \left[ \frac{b \times b^3}{12} + (b \times b) \times b^2 \right] \\ & = b^4 \left[ \frac{1}{4} + 2 \left[ \frac{1}{12} + b \right] \right] \\ & = \frac{29b^4}{12} \end{aligned} $

The elastic section modulus $ Z_e $ is then :

$ Z_e = \frac{\frac{29b^4}{12}}{\frac{3b}{2}} = \frac{29b^4}{12} \times \frac{2}{3b} = \frac{29}{18} b^3 $

The plastic section modulus $ Z_P $ is calculated as :

$ Z_P = \frac{A}{2} \left( \bar{y}_1 + \bar{y}_2 \right) $

The total area $ A $ is :

$ A = 3b \times b + 2 \times b^2 = 5b^2 $

Calculate the first moment of area $\bar{y}_1$ as follows :

$ \bar{y}_1 = \frac{\left( 3b \times \frac{b}{2} \right) \times \frac{b}{4} + (b^2 \times b)}{3b \times \frac{b}{2} + b^2} = \frac{b \left[ \frac{3}{8} + 1 \right]}{\left[ \frac{3}{2} + 1 \right]} = \frac{11}{20} b $

The plastic section modulus $Z_p$ is then :

$ Z_p = \frac{A}{2} \left( \bar{y}_1 + \bar{y}_2 \right) = \frac{5b^2}{2} \left( \frac{11}{20} b + \frac{11}{20} b \right) = \frac{11}{4} b^3 $

The shape factor (S.F.) is calculated as :

$ S.F. = \frac{\frac{11}{4}b^3}{\frac{29}{18}b^3} = \frac{11 \times 18}{4 \times 29} = 1.706 = 1.71 $

Hence, the correct option is (B).

content: The cross-section of a girder is shown in the figure (not to scale). The section is symmetric about a vertical axis (Y - Y). The moment of inertia of the section about the horizontal axis (X - X) passing through the centroid is _______ cm4 (round off to nearest integer).
explanation:
$$ \begin{aligned} & A_1=40 \times 10=400 \mathrm{~cm}^2 \\\\ & A_2=20 \times 50=1000 \mathrm{~cm}^2 \\\ & \bar{y}_1=50+5=55 \mathrm{~cm} \\\ & \bar{y}_2=\frac{50}{2}=25 \mathrm{~cm} \end{aligned} $$

Centroid of composite shape from bottom fibre :

$\begin{aligned} & \bar{y}=\frac{A_1 \bar{y}_1+A_2 \overline{y_2}}{A_1+A_2}=\frac{(400 \times 55)+(1000 \times 25)}{400+1000}=33.5714 \mathrm{~cm} \\\\ & I_{x x}=\left[40 \times \frac{10^3}{12}+400\left(\bar{y}_1-\bar{y}_2\right)^2\right]+\left[20 \times \frac{50^3}{12}+1000\left(\bar{y}-\bar{y}_2\right)^2\right]\end{aligned}$

$$ \begin{aligned} & I_{x x}=\left[40 \times \frac{10^3}{12}+400(55-33.5714)^2\right]+\left[20 \times \frac{50^3}{12}+1000(33.5714-25)^2\right] \\\\ & I_{x x}=187007.29+281802.23 \\\\ & I_{x x}=468809.52 \mathrm{~cm}^4 \cong 468810 \mathrm{~cm}^4 \end{aligned} $$

Hence, the correct answer is 468810 .

content: A disc of radius $$'r'$$ has a hole of radius $$'r/2'$$ cut-out as shown. The centroid of the remaining disc (shaded portion) at a radial distance from the center $$''O''$$ is
For the section shown below, second moment of the area about an axis $$d/4$$ distance above the bottom of the area is
